transcript_text:
spk_0: Okay, so, Alexandra, tell me a bit about yourself. What are you currently doing? Are you studying? Have you graduated... jobs... things like that?
spk_1: Yes, so I graduated in 2024. I did a law degree, and I'm currently working at the university that I graduated from. I'm doing an administrator role, and that's with the university legal clinic. So, yeah... that's what I'm doing at the moment.
spk_0: Fantastic! And you said 2024... you graduated?
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: So, walk me through what a typical week looks like, then, for you.
spk_1: Yes. So, a typical week: I work three days at the legal clinic in Stratford, and then I work two days in Bloomsbury for the... like the Centre for Access to Justice. So, that's more helping out with student pro bono opportunities and student support and that kind of thing. And the work that I do at the clinic in Stratford is things like supervising students, taking inquiries, signposting... all that sort of thing. So, it's like two kind of part-time roles rolled into one.
spk_0: Okay, so who do you currently live with at the moment, then?
spk_1: I live with my parents and my family... Yeah, fantastic!
spk_0: So, with living with your family, I know that comes with interesting dynamics... So, who is currently responsible for the food in your household?
spk_1: We kind of share responsibilities, so it's shared quite equally between myself and my parents.
spk_0: Okay, perfect. And then, when you're eating... do you normally eat as a family together in the evenings, or do you do that separately?
spk_1: We normally eat together... yeah. Perfect!
spk_0: Okay... gonna switch it up a little bit. Want to ask a bit about social media. What social media platforms are you currently on?
spk_1: I'm currently on Instagram. I do have Facebook, but I don't really use it very much. I also have LinkedIn, but again, I don't use it so much. I would say the... the primary one that I use is Instagram. Yeah, great!
spk_0: And what about YouTube, Snapchat... things like that? X?
spk_1: Yeah, I don't use X or Snapchat. I do use YouTube occasionally, but yeah, not... not as frequently, I would say.
spk_0: Okay, if you were then able to rank them from what you use most to least, how would you order them?
spk_1: So, I would say I use Instagram most, and then probably like YouTube, LinkedIn, Facebook... Yeah, and the others I don't use.
spk_0: Okay, perfect. And do you know roughly how much time you spend on a typical day on social media?
spk_1: Probably, like, an hour... Okay, yeah.
spk_0: Where, then, would you say that you see the most food-related content online?
spk_1: Yeah, I would say that I see quite a lot of food-related content on Instagram.
spk_0: And do you follow any food influencers, creators, local food accounts... things like that?
spk_1: I do follow some creators that, like, make some food content, but it's not their primary thing. I'm vegan, so I follow some, like, vegan creators, and sometimes they talk about food.
spk_0: Okay, that's good! So, walk me through a typical weekday, then... from morning to night, for you. What do you eat, when, and where?
spk_1: So, typically for like breakfast, I'll either have just like toast at home for breakfast, or if I'm like running late, I'll just kind of skip it. I normally bring like a packed lunch for work that will either be like leftovers from dinner the night before, or I'll just like make a sandwich with like vegetables and hummus and that kind of thing, or like maybe a salad, and then dinner I have at home. Kind of take it in turns with my family, like who cooks? So I'll cook like a couple nights a week. And yeah, it will just be something with like tofu or beans or like something that's trying to be healthy, you know.
spk_0: So, how does that differ, then, between the weekends and your... and your weekdays?
spk_1: I'd say in... At the weekend, I'm much more likely to, like, eat breakfast, but again, I don't tend to put that much effort into it. But I'll probably have, like, cereal or porridge or something like that. And I think I probably, like, snack a bit more during the weekend and probably eat more kind of, like, treat food, more like crisps and, like, chocolate and biscuits and stuff. But in terms of, like, the kind of lunches and dinners, that's kind of pretty much the same, really, apart from I sometimes have, like, a roast dinner on... On a weekend? Yeah!
spk_0: Okay, perfect! So, how often, then, would you say you are cooking?
spk_1: Yes, I... I would say probably, like... probably like three times a week. Yeah.
spk_0: And what kind of meals is that? Is that like meal prep? Is it just like a meal for that evening? Like, what's it look like?
spk_1: Yeah, mostly it will just be like a meal for the evening, but sometimes I do try and do a little bit extra so I can take it for lunch the next day.
spk_0: And what does cooking mean to you?
spk_1: I do quite like cooking sometimes, but I often feel like it is a bit of a chore just because, like, you have to do it so regularly. I think I would like to be a bit more organized with it and maybe do some more meal prep and stuff, but just never kind of get around to doing it and getting in the habit of it. But yeah... so, some days I'm kind of more bothered to put more effort in than others. I would say that's fair.
spk_0: I think we're all like that...
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: Okay... so, how often, then, are you ordering food or groceries for delivery?
spk_1: I would say maybe, like, a couple of times or, like, three times a month, maybe.
spk_0: Is that for takeaways, or is that for groceries?
spk_1: For takeaways... yeah, I haven't ordered groceries for delivery before.
spk_0: So, which meals, then, would you say feel the most stressful for you?
spk_1: I would say dinner, definitely, because I feel like I have to put in some more effort, and because most of the time I'll be cooking for other people as well. Whereas, like lunch, I just do that for myself... I don't do anyone else's.
spk_0: That makes sense... Which meals, then, feel the most enjoyable for you?
spk_1: Most enjoyable is when I have, like, leftover food for lunch because I haven't had to put in more effort during the day. And it's nice because I did put in the effort earlier.
spk_0: And when does food feel functional, social, and comforting?
spk_1: Functional? I guess just like a normal weekday because, you know, you have to eat. Enjoyable is when, like, I feel like cooking, and I make something that's nice, and I enjoy it. That's always good. And yeah, I like meeting up with friends and, or like, family as well, and like going out and eating or like getting a takeaway... that's very, like, sociable and enjoyable, I would say.
spk_0: Okay. And what about groceries, then? How often do you typically do your... your grocery shopping?
spk_1: I would say I probably do, like, the main shop or, like, contribute to the main shop, like, once a week. But then I'll do, like, a few top-up shops. I live pretty close to, like, small, like, convenience supermarkets, so it's just easy, like, to pop in and get stuff.
spk_0: Which... which supermarket is closest to you?
spk_1: The co-op.
spk_0: So, obviously, you mentioned big and some top-up shops. What does a top-up shop look like, and what does the big shop look like in terms of cost and sort of size of baskets?
spk_1: So, top-up shops will be things... it'll be like snacks, and if there's something specific that I need that I've run out of, like for a recipe or something... So, it'll just be kind of all like random ingredients, and it'll just be like £5 or £10, something like that. And then, like, a big shop will probably be for the family, maybe like £50. Again, it's not massive because I don't buy meat or anything. So, vegetables are quite cheap.
spk_0: Fair play... So, roughly how much would you say you spend on food in a typical week?
spk_1: I would spend maybe... like for myself. Because if I'm thinking... because, like, let me tell you about the big shop, that would be kind of like split between my family. So, like, what I'm paying for is probably like 20 pounds, maybe.
spk_0: Okay, perfect. And do you have a set budget for each week, or does it change?
spk_1: I don't have a set budget, no.
spk_0: So, in total then, what are you spending across food deliveries, groceries, and eating out in a week?
spk_1: Probably like 35 pounds a week, maybe...
spk_0: Okay, so... 20 of that goes to your groceries. Where does the rest of it sit?
spk_1: Yeah... so if I'm thinking of, like, the cost of takeaways and, like, spreading that out across the month, I suppose that if... breaking it down by week. Yeah.
spk_0: Oh, as in you... only you don't get a takeaway per week, so you're splitting the cost.
spk_1: Yeah, that's fine.
spk_0: I get what you're saying... Okay, that's great! So, what reason, then, do you normally order food for delivery? So... takeaways.
spk_1: If I really don't feel like cooking, or if I just, like, fancy, like, a treat, or it might be, like, a special occasion or something... and if I feel like I don't want to go out to a restaurant but want to have restaurant food... I suppose, yeah.
spk_0: Perfect. Okay. And then, is there a day of the week that you're more likely to order a takeaway?
spk_1: It would probably be like a Friday, I guess...
spk_0: Why a Friday?
spk_1: Well, I suppose it's the end of the week... It's just a bit more relaxed. You can spend a bit more time eating. Yes... more kind of social.
spk_0: Does delivery feel like a treat, a habit, or just part of normal spending for you?
spk_1: I think it does feel like a treat. I think I try not to get it too much and let it become like a habit because then I don't think it would feel as, like, special... and because it is quite a lot of money. So, I do think of it more as a treat.
spk_0: Okay. Okay. I'd love for you to tell me about the last time you ordered food for delivery via one of the apps like Just Eat, Delivery, or Uber. So, paint me a picture of what was going on that day. What triggered the order, who were you with? And ultimately, why did you decide to...?
spk_1: The place was actually... Yesterday, I ordered delivery from Deliveroo. It was because... So, I really... I couldn't be bothered to cook, and I didn't really have much food in the fridge. My mum wanted to try this new place that she, like, read about in a book that had good reviews, but we didn't really want to go out because it's, like, a weeknight. So... yeah, just made the order on Deliveroo.
spk_0: So, was it a planned or a spontaneous order?
spk_1: Yeah... it was spontaneous.
spk_0: And what would you say... triggered it?
spk_1: Probably because my mum had, like, found this place, and she was like, 'Oh, we might as well... let's just try it out.' Yeah.
spk_0: Okay... and what kind of foods, then, do you typically order when you're getting takeaway?
spk_1: Well, yesterday that was a new place that we ordered from, which was... it was a South Indian restaurant. But normally I order... the main place I go to is Tofu Vegan. It's like a Chinese vegan restaurant, and it's really good, and everything's vegan. So, I often order from there.
spk_0: Great. And do you have, like, a go-to order?
spk_1: Yeah, I do from there. There's like a... like set dishes that I know that I like and I go for. Yeah.
spk_0: And would... would I know you said you don't do it weekly, so would the majority of your orders then be placed at that restaurant and have the same meal?
spk_1: Yeah, probably.
spk_0: Okay... do you think your ordering habits have changed over time?
spk_1: Yeah, because I didn't really use to order much takeaway before, and also I wasn't... I've only been vegan for like six months, so I would... if I would order, I would order from like different places. Yeah. So it kind of... it does change like where I order from. Yeah.
spk_0: Interesting... How come you weren't ordering as many takeaways before?
spk_1: What's changed before? Well, I didn't have a job, so I didn't have much disposable income... so that was probably the main reason.
spk_0: Yeah, makes total sense. Okay, was that post-uni then, that like... period between uni and... and getting your... your first job?
spk_1: Yeah... and during uni as well? I didn't really order out much.
spk_0: Fair. So, you mentioned you don't, or you haven't, ordered groceries for... for delivery. Have you ever considered ordering groceries through one of the delivery apps?
spk_1: Not really, because I live close to a shop, so it just wouldn't really make sense, I don't think.
spk_0: Can you think of a time, maybe, when grocery delivery would have made your life easier?
spk_1: Not really, because I've always... because the shop is like a 5-minute walk away, and I've always been able to... to walk, so.
spk_0: Fair... Okay. What would make the perfect grocery delivery app for students?
spk_1: For students? I think if you can get groceries... because sometimes, like, it's obviously limited what you can get in terms of, like, what you can get ordered. So if there's, like, a wide range of groceries that you can get, and if the... it needs to be, like... I mean, it needs to be a quick delivery time, I suppose. Yeah.
spk_0: Okay, great! And does grocery shopping feel overwhelming or stressful for you?
spk_1: Yeah, sometimes if it's... the shop's quite busy, and yeah, sometimes there's like music that can be a bit... kind of loud. Yeah, absolutely!
spk_0: Okay... have you seen any promotions for grocery delivery recently?
spk_1: I don't know if I've seen it recently, but I have seen ads, I think, for grocery delivery.
spk_0: What did you think of those... those ads?
spk_1: I mean, when I first saw it... because it was like a new... When it was like a new thing, I guess I thought it's quite a good idea. Like, if you can't... you know, you... You can't really leave the house, or like, you... Your supermarket is a little bit far, and you need groceries. Yeah, I think it's like... good to have.
spk_0: Did it ever make you consider ordering the groceries...?
spk_1: Not really... because, like, again, it's just me living very close to a shop that I can walk to.
spk_0: Would promotions and discounts make you consider grocery delivery?
spk_1: Yeah, I think so. So, if there was, like, a money-off deal or something which would make the delivery cheaper than me going to the shop myself, then I would definitely, like, consider it.
spk_0: And when you say, 'going to the shop yourself,' what do you mean?
spk_1: I mean, like, walking to the shop and buying the food in the shop...
spk_0: So, the whole cost of the goods and delivery has to be cheaper than what it would be in the supermarket that you walk to?
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: Great. Okay, I'd like you to think back, then, to when you were a student... Did you use any delivery apps then?
spk_1: Yeah... occasionally. Yeah... sometimes.
spk_0: Which... which services did you use?
spk_1: I think I use Deliveroo and UberEats... I've used.
spk_0: Okay... and were you aware of the student benefits during your time at uni?
spk_1: I don't think so...
spk_0: Okay, so, for example, Deliveroo has a Deliveroo Plus Silver, which is free for students. And then Uber One has reduced subscription fees for students as well.
spk_1: Oh no! I didn't know that.
spk_0: Okay, interesting... Would you say your order habits have changed since you've begun working full-time?
spk_1: Yeah, I order more now... I would say.
spk_0: And is that simply down just to the financial element?
spk_1: Yeah... I would say so.
spk_0: You mentioned at the start... I think the delivery is the go-to, and I'm interested to know if that was the same when you were at uni.
spk_1: I think it was probably split between, like, UberEats and Deliveroo... whatever. Yeah. If there was a certain deal on, I suppose.
spk_0: Okay... so it used to be split between the two of them, and now it's mainly delivery.
spk_1: Yeah, I think so. I think it's just worked out like that in terms of... in terms of, I think, because the place... the places that I order from quite a lot often have better deals, I think, on delivery. I don't, like, have a particular preference for, like, one service or the other. It's just whatever's got the better deal. You see what I mean?
spk_0: Yeah, absolutely. What is it, do you think, that makes you order more often on Delivery than on Uber?
spk_1: Yeah, so it's... I think for the... the restaurant, like, the restaurant I order most from—Tofu, vegan—they often, on delivery, will have like a buy one, get one free deal. And I don't think I haven't seen that on UberEats, so that's why I go through delivery.
spk_0: Okay... apologies. I've jumped ahead and asked you a number of other questions, so I'm just figuring out where I need to go. Okay, so... what would you say tends to matter most to you in the moment when you order?
spk_1: What matters the most?
spk_0: The.
spk_1: I think... the price... yeah.
spk_0: I have... I have actually five options that I want you to consider as well. So, you've got: price, which is top offers and promotions; speed; habit; and subscription benefits. Where would you rank those from most important to you to least?
spk_1: Yeah, so I'd say price is most important, and then I guess offers and promotions... speed. And then, what are the other ones?
spk_0: Habit and subscription benefits?
spk_1: Yeah, I would say habit... and then subscription benefit at the bottom.
spk_0: Okay, I'd love for you, then, to rate how well delivery and Uber Eats provide those on a scale of 1 to 5, with 1 being extremely terrible and 5 being extremely good. So, firstly, then, delivery price out of five.
spk_1: So... sorry, do you mean, like, each?
spk_0: Yes... we'll go through all the lists. If we're scaling them one to five—one being really bad, five being really good—why would you rate delivery on price out of five?
spk_1: I would rate it a four.
spk_0: Okay... it's pretty good. And what about Uber?
spk_1: A three.
spk_0: Okay... and then, what about delivery on offers and promotions?
spk_1: A4... I would say.
spk_0: And Uber?
spk_1: A3.
spk_0: And then... delivery speed?
spk_1: Five... I think it's pretty speedy.
spk_0: Okay... and then, Uber?
spk_1: Four, I think...
spk_0: And then... habit.
spk_1: Habit...? A5, I would say.
spk_0: And then... Uber?
spk_1: A4.
spk_0: And then... subscription benefits on delivery.
spk_1: I'd say... a three.
spk_0: And then... Uber.
spk_1: 3. As well... okay.
spk_0: So... pretty even on the subscription benefits?
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: Great. So, if an app has the restaurant you want, but another app is cheaper, what do you normally do? So, let's say in this case... obviously, delivery is your, your go-to.
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: Your favorite takeaway is on there, and it's 2 pounds cheaper on UberEats.
spk_1: Yeah... what do you order on Uber Eats?
spk_0: Okay. Yeah, a two-pound... You... you would assess that that's enough free to make this. Okay. What if it was one point?
spk_1: Yeah, I'd still go for the cheapo one.
spk_0: Okay. And would you usually price compare if there are multiple apps and restaurants?
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: Okay... so every time you do an order, you look at the cost on delivery and then compare it with Uber.
spk_1: Yeah.
spk_0: Okay... and how often, then, do you find that Uber wins over delivery?
spk_1: Not that often, but I think it's because of the place... like, if there's just a couple places that I'm ordering from, I think it just happens to be that one of them generally has better deals on delivery. Yeah.
spk_0: Great! Does speed and reliability matter to you?
spk_1: Yes.
spk_0: And how do you... how would you rate that with... with delivery? Like, do you find them reliable when they say that they're going to be there at a certain time? Do they... do they meet that expectation?
spk_1: Yeah, I think they are reliable. I mean, I think often on the app, like... the estimated time of arrival will change by, like, five minutes or whatever, but I think it's, like, pretty fast, and I don't think it could be much faster, so... yeah.
spk_0: Great. Okay. So, you mentioned you're not subscribed to any... any of the services at the moment. Is that correct?
spk_1: Yeah, that's right.
spk_0: Yeah... Do you think there's anything that would make you subscribe?
spk_1: I'm not sure. I'm... I'm not really the kind of person that likes having subscriptions anyway because I just don't... like, like money coming out. Like, I prefer to pay for things, like, up front, like the... the full amount. So yeah, like having a bit of money come out of my account every month... I don't really like it. And also, I would worry that I would then feel like I need to use the app more, and I don't really want to encourage myself to eat more takeaways.
spk_0: That's... yeah, totally fair. I get it. How do you personally decide, then, whether a food delivery subscription is worth it?
spk_1: I mean, like, if it was the kind of deal where I would feel like it's not that much money, and I'm going to save money because I know that I won't feel pressure to, like, order more to kind of make up for the subscription... you know, the money that I've spent... then yeah, maybe I would consider it. But I don't want to have to, like, change my habits, if you see what I mean.
spk_0: Yeah... does free delivery change how often, or how easily, you would order?
spk_1: Yeah, I think the kind of, like, total price affects what I decide to do rather than, like, specifically free delivery, because it doesn't really matter, like, how you get the money off... if it's, like, you know, a percentage off of the food or if it's free delivery. It just kind of matters to me what the total price is.
spk_0: Makes sense. Okay, thank you. If you think about Delivery Plus and then Uber One, what would be the difference for you, in your mind?
spk_1: I don't really know what the difference is... to be honest with you.
spk_0: Okay, great! Do you think you'd be more likely to choose one over the other?
spk_1: I would only say Deliveroo just because of how I feel like right now... that the way I use it, the office is generally better. But I would have to look at the offers, and I would just pick whatever one was better, I suppose.
spk_0: Okay, thank you. So, I'm going to read out some of the benefits, then, of a food delivery subscription, and then I'd ask you to rank them from most important to least important to you. So, the first benefit is free delivery. The second one is always on offer: 10% off your favorite takeaway. Always on grocery offers, retail partners like Boots, and then other partnerships like your transport and lifestyle discounts.
spk_1: I would say the most important is things being on offer... Then, I would say free delivery. I would say the, like... what was it? Lifestyle discount... something.
spk_0: Yes.
spk_1: Would come next... I've forgotten what the other ones...
spk_0: The other two left are grocery offers... and the retail partners, like Boots.
spk_1: Yeah... then I would say grocery offers, and last, the retail partners.
spk_0: Interesting... Okay, so why was the 'always on' offers your top choice?
spk_1: Because I think that's the kind of thing that I look out for the most... Yeah, getting the best price is what I'm mainly concerned with, I think.
spk_0: And then your least relevant one was the retail partners, like Boots... Why was that least relevant to you?
spk_1: I just think that... I don't know, that I just wouldn't be, like, ordering from places like Boots on a food delivery app, I suppose.
spk_0: What is it about that sort of an order that will put you off on an app like that?
spk_1: I just think of it... Yeah. I think it's just because I use these apps for, like, takeaways and haven't really used them for groceries. Yeah, I'm just not in, kind of, the habit of doing that.
spk_0: Okay... and are there any benefits that weren't listed that you'd like to have?
spk_1: No, I don't think so...
spk_0: What, then, would make a food delivery subscription feel like it was actually designed for students?
spk_1: Designed for students? If it... if there was, like, you know, very good kind of promotions, you know, offers, money off, that kind of thing... it's always like what students are looking for, I think. Yeah, that would be the main thing, I'd say.
spk_0: And then, beyond food, what were your favorite brands to use as a student?
spk_1: Beyond food... So, do you mean like clothes and that kind of stuff?
spk_0: Yeah... any brand like that.
spk_1: Difficult... I... What did I do as a tune? I mainly bought clothes and stuff, like secondhand. So I use, like, Vinted and that kind of thing, and eBay and places like that a lot. So, yeah, I probably spent quite a lot of money on Vinted.
spk_0: Okay, so Vent is a good one. Well, thinking about... then, the... the perfect brand collaborations, which brand do you think would work best with delivery? UberEats and Just Eat?
spk_1: I'm not really sure what you mean... in terms of brands.
spk_0: Yeah. So, like, anybody that can collaborate... I don't know, you might have seen in the UK, Greg's collaborated with a jeweler, for example.
spk_1: Oh, I see...
spk_0: So, like, things like that... that's maybe just a little bit quirky and would also kind of make sense.
spk_1: Oh, in terms of, like, what students like... Yeah, maybe, like, I don't know, Nike or, like, trainers... like those sorts of things.
spk_0: Which... which app would you associate that with?
spk_1: Maybe more... Uber?
spk_0: Okay... why Uber?
spk_1: I feel like... I don't know if it's because of the cars or because, like, it's a very, like, young person thing to, like, get an Uber.
spk_0: Okay. And what about delivering... just the delivery? Any... any brands that you would associate with them for... for a good collab, or something you would consider a good collab?
spk_1: I don't know... It's quite difficult. Yeah, I'm not too sure.
spk_0: Okay, that's okay. We can skip that one. That's no problem. Okay, so we want to switch up to rider receptions then... So, what are your general perceptions of delivery app riders?
spk_1: What are my perceptions? I think, in my experience, they've been very reliable and speedy... So, yeah.
spk_0: Okay... and do you know anyone who is, or has been, a writer?
spk_1: No, I don't know anyone.
spk_0: Okay... would you have ever considered being a writer yourself?
spk_1: No, because I... I don't like the idea of cycling on the road, so I couldn't do it, fair.
spk_0: Do you have any concerns about becoming a writer?
spk_1: Oh yeah, I would think it was, like, quite dangerous and probably quite stressful, and so you probably don't get paid enough.
spk_0: So, in your opinion, how would you say it compares to other part-time jobs or side hustles?
spk_1: Oh, it's probably a really, like, hard part-time job... Yeah.
spk_0: What I want to do is... I want to show you an advertisement for becoming a writer. I'm going to share my screen with you and just get your opinion on whether or not... you scroll back to the top. Do you think this would appeal to students?
spk_1: Yeah. Okay. I see how they're, like, advertising it to students in terms of, like, it's flexible and you can do whatever hours you want, which, I suppose, is good. I feel like the picture they chose was not of students, so maybe that's not the best... I don't know. Or, like, slightly older people. But yeah, I can understand the kind of... the sort of flexibility angle being appealing for students.
spk_0: Do you find it... do you find the ad appealing if you were a student now?
spk_1: I mean, not to me personally, because I wouldn't consider, as I said, the cycling... but I think, I think students might find it appealing. Yeah. Others, others that aren't scared.
spk_0: Why, why do you think they, they might find it appealing then if, if the fear wasn't there?
